content.claim: Poland's partial reversal of its successful 1999 comprehensive reform (delaying vocational tracking) correlates with PISA 2022 performance decline, but the decline is confounded by COVID-19 pandemic learning loss in the same PISA cycle
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Causal attribution is explicitly confounded; source itself frames decline as joint COVID/reversal effect
Single country case with no control group; cannot isolate re-tracking effect from pandemic learning disruption
No within-cohort tracking on pandemic vs. structural effects
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
